author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
The Phenix After Death Coming to Life Again.
title: The Phenix After Death Coming to Life Again.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Now, what you say about the phenix has nothing whatever to do with the subject before us.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: For the phenix symbolizes the resurrection of the body; it does not do away with the sex of souls; if indeed, as is thought, he is born afresh after his death.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: I suppose, however, that you thought your discourse would not be sufficiently plausible unless you declaimed a good deal about the phenix, after the fashion of young people.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Now do you find in the body of your bird male organs of generation and not a male bird; or female ones, and not a female?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: But, I beg of you, reflect on what it is you say,—what theory you are trying to construct, and to recommend for our acceptance.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: You say that the soul, spread through all the limbs of the body, grew stiff by congelation, and received the entire shape of the whole body from the crown of the head to the soles of the feet, and from the inmost marrow to the skin’s outward surface.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: At this rate it must have received, in the case of a female body, all the inner appurtenances of a woman’s body, and yet not be a woman!

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Why, pray, are all the members feminine in a true living body, and yet the whole no woman?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: And why all be male, and the result not a man?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Who can be so presumptuous as to believe, and profess, and teach all this?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Is it that souls never generate?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Then, of course, mules and she-mules are not male and female.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Is it that souls without bodies of flesh would be unable to cohabit?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Well, but this deprivation is shared by castrated men; and yet, although both the process and the motion be taken from them, their sex is not removed—some slender remnant of their male members being still left to them.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Nobody ever said that a eunuch is not a male.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: What now becomes of your opinion, that the souls even of eunuchs have the generative organs unimpaired, and that these organs will remain entire, on your principle, in their souls, even when they are clean removed from their bodily structure?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: For you say, the soul knows how to withdraw itself when that part of the flesh begins to be cut off, so that the form which has been removed when amputated is not lost; but although spread over it by condensation, it retires by an extremely rapid movement, and so buries itself within as to be kept quite safe; yet that cannot, forsooth, be a male in the other world which carries with it thither the whole appendage of male organs of generation, and which, if it had not even other signs in the body, was a male by reason of those organs alone.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: These opinions, my son, have no truth in them; if you will not allow that there is sex in the soul, there cannot be a body either.
